--- SOURCE 2: Plant-Made Pharmaceuticals Background and Key Points ---
URL: https://archive.bio.org/articles/plant-made-pharmaceuticals-background-and-key-points

SUMMARY:
Plant-Made Pharmaceuticals Background and Key Points New advances in biotechnology make it possible to turn plants into "factories" that produce therapeutic proteins for use in the manufacture of biotech drugs, medicines and therapies. Plant-made pharmaceuticals (PMPs) are the result of an innovative application of biotechnology to plants to enable them to produce therapeutic proteins that could ultimately be used by the medical community to combat life-threatening illnesses, such as heart disease, cancer, HIV, diabetes, Alzheimer's disease and cystic fibrosis. Plant-made pharmaceutical production is regulated under stringent requirements of the U.S. Department of Agriculture (USDA) and the Food and Drug Administration (FDA). Developing therapeutic proteins in plants can be a safer, more efficient and cost-effective method of protein production.

FULL CONTENT:
Plant-Made Pharmaceuticals Background and Key Points

For centuries, plants have served as a natural source of treatments and therapies. New advances in biotechnology make it possible to turn plants into "factories" that produce therapeutic proteins for use in the manufacture of biotech drugs, medicines and therapies. Plant-made pharmaceuticals (PMPs) are the result of an innovative application of biotechnology to plants to enable them to produce therapeutic proteins that could ultimately be used by the medical community to combat life-threatening illnesses, such as heart disease, cancer, HIV, diabetes, Alzheimer's disease and cystic fibrosis. PMP technology to develop therapeutic proteins is a safe, efficient and cost-effective alternative to traditional methods using microbial fermentation or animal cell cultures. Plant-made pharmaceuticals have the potential to provide patients with the benefits of greater and faster access to medicines.

The Biotechnology Industry Organization (BIO) and its members are committed to protecting human health and the environment by ensuring the safety of PMPs during all stages of development and production through a close working relationship with the regulatory agencies on current and future regulations to assure they are both rigorous and enforceable.

Key Points

Plant-made pharmaceutical production is regulated under stringent requirements of the U.S. Department of Agriculture (USDA) and the Food and Drug Administration (FDA).

Developing therapeutic proteins in plants can be a safer, more efficient and cost-effective method of protein production.

--- SOURCE 3: Plant manufactured pharmaceuticals ---
URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Plant_manufactured_pharmaceuticals

SUMMARY:
Plant manufactured Pharmaceuticals are pharmaceuticals derived from genetically modified plants used as therapeutic compounds.

FULL CONTENT:
![](/static/images/icons/wikipedia.png)
![Wikipedia](/static/images/mobile/copyright/wikipedia-wordmark-en.svg)

## Contents

# Plant manufactured pharmaceuticals

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | This article **needs additional citations for [verification](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability")**. Please help [improve this article](/wiki/Special:EditPage/Plant_manufactured_pharmaceuticals "Special:EditPage/Plant manufactured pharmaceuticals") by [adding citations to reliable sources](/wiki/Help:Referencing_for_beginners "Help:Referencing for beginners"). Unsourced material may be challenged and removed. *Find sources:* ["Plant manufactured pharmaceuticals"](https://www.google.com/search?as_eq=wikipedia&q=%22Plant+manufactured+pharmaceuticals%22) – [news](https://www.google.com/search?tbm=nws&q=%22Plant+manufactured+pharmaceuticals%22+-wikipedia&tbs=ar:1) **·** [newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Plant+manufactured+pharmaceuticals%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Plant+manufactured+pharmaceuticals%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Plant+manufactured+pharmaceuticals%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Plant+manufactured+pharmaceuticals%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(July 2013)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/9/99/Question_book-new.svg/60px-Question_book-new.svg.png)

**Plant manufactured Pharmaceuticals** are pharmaceuticals derived from genetically modified plants used as therapeutic compounds. This can be used as the replacement for the traditional method of inoculating animals for [Cell Culture](/wiki/Cell_culture "Cell culture") production. We can use plants to cure and prevent diseases that may have once been deemed incurable. Through biotechnological advancements, we are able to produce complex therapeutic proteins from plant cells.[[1]](#cite_note-Susannah_Horton-1) Such [Therapeutic Proteins](/wiki/Protein_therapy "Protein therapy") are seen in brands like Enevrel and [Remicade](/wiki/Infliximab "Infliximab") for rheumatoid arthritis, Herceptin, a breast cancer treatment. Plants like tobacco are hosts for protein production for applications such as; anemia, hepatitis C & B, hypertension, antimicrobial, and liver disease.[[1]](#cite_note-Susannah_Horton-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## Impact on business and industry

With the advancement of Plant Manufactured Pharmaceuticals, comes the advancements of a new type production in industry. Companies such as [ZEA Biosciences](https://zeabio.com/) are developing cost-effective and scalable pharmaceutical ingredients using plants instead of cell culture. Unlike cell culture, plants can have a much larger production capacity, a mass quantity of plant hosts on site, and the ability to make specific antibodies that is used as a bio-reactor for specific patient needs.[[1]](#cite_note-Susannah_Horton-1) Indirectly, the need to grow plants that are being used as Plant Manufactured Pharmaceuticals will increase in geographic areas where certain plants naturally grow, for instance in developing countries. Increasing the need for agricultural societies in developing countries will help certain countries to export and make trade alliance with other countries and with the development of the therapies that can control diseases like [Cholera](/wiki/Cholera "Cholera") and [HIV/AIDS](/wiki/HIV/AIDS "HIV/AIDS").[[1]](#cite_note-Susannah_Horton-1)

## Ideas of enhanced recovery

Landscape Gardens that must be grown for the production of the therapeutic proteins brings a new time of recovery for certain patients. Professor Roger Ulrich of Texas A&M University believes that [Therapeutic Gardens](/wiki/Therapeutic_garden "Therapeutic garden") can help the spiritual needs of patients and enhance stress recovery. This relieves the patient of stress and gives the patient a feeling of tranquility during their recovery.[[1]](#cite_note-Susannah_Horton-1)

## Criticism and awareness

Many corporations are allowed to create [genetically modified organisms](/wiki/Genetically_modified_organism "Genetically modified organism") and secure them through [intellectual property](/wiki/Intellectual_property "Intellectual property") rights creating monopolies, a fact that continues to evoke criticism . Awareness and education is needed for the public to understand how even GM plants have helped medical research. For instance, in 1992, a group of American students produced a [Hepatitis B](/wiki/Hepatitis_B "Hepatitis B") vaccine from a genetically modified [tobacco](/wiki/Tobacco "Tobacco") plant showing the ability of produce pharmaceutical compounds.[[1]](#cite_note-Susannah_Horton-1)

enriched_points.expanded_content: Plant-Made Pharmaceuticals (PMPs) are therapeutic proteins produced using genetically modified plants, serving as an innovative and sustainable biofactory for drug development.
PMPs provide a cost-effective, scalable, and efficient alternative to traditional methods, enhancing access to essential medicines for various life-threatening diseases.
Genetically modified plants are engineered to produce complex therapeutic proteins, enabling advancements in treatments for illnesses like cancer, diabetes, and HIV/AIDS.
